OCR Use: OCR-dependent: OCR text and layout are required inputs and are fused with image features in the TILT-style encoder-decoder.
Rationale / Key Technique: Combines OCR text, layout, and image features in a TILT-style encoder-decoder, extending it with sparse long-context processing and layer-wise multimodal fusion.

OCR Use: No OCR is used; evidence retrieval and answer generation operate over page images and visual embeddings.
Rationale / Key Technique: Uses page images directly with a lightweight visual retriever, then adaptively selects evidence pages before passing them to a frozen VLM generator.

OCR Use: Uses OCR selectively after visual evidence localization, but it is not OCR-dependent because the model directly consumes page images and performs end-to-end locate-then-read reasoning.
Rationale / Key Technique: Teaches a locate-then-reason chain in which the model visually grounds relevant regions and selectively reads textual evidence.

OCR Use: OCR-dependent: an OCR engine extracts text chunks that drive coarse-to-fine retrieval before multimodal answer generation.
Rationale / Key Technique: Retrieves OCR chunks, reranks evidence, and combines the selected textual evidence with multimodal page representations for answer generation.

OCR Use: No OCR is used; retrieval is based on visual page embeddings with neighboring-page context fusion.
Rationale / Key Technique: Improves visual page retrieval by fusing neighboring-page context into page embeddings, targeting evidence selection without an OCR-first pipeline.

OCR Use: No OCR is used; it explicitly uses OCR-free thumbnails, visual fetch actions, and visual working memory.
Rationale / Key Technique: Uses OCR-free page thumbnails and visual fetch actions with working memory; key technique is coarse-to-fine agentic evidence aggregation trained with SFT and GRPO.

OCR Use: Uses OCR-derived document content for outline/search and text evidence, but is not fully OCR-dependent because it also retrieves and verifies page, image, chart, and table evidence through visual tools.
Rationale / Key Technique: Builds a tree-style document outline and coordinates actor/reviewer behavior with tools and memory to navigate multimodal long documents.

OCR Use: Uses extracted text that may include OCR-derived strings through the document processing/read tools, but it is not OCR-dependent because the agent also reads images, tables, and page screenshots; OCR is mainly contrasted with baselines.
Rationale / Key Technique: Trains an open-source ReAct document agent with search/read tools and exploration-then-synthesis supervision for grounded information seeking.

OCR Use: OCR-dependent: OCR/layout parsing supplies text and element structure used by the page navigator, localizer, answer sampler, and adjudicator agents.
Rationale / Key Technique: Uses OCR/layout parsing and visual element localization through page navigator, element localizer, answer sampler, and adjudicator agents.

OCR Use: Uses OCR only indirectly: training/evaluation discuss built-in OCR capability, OCR-oriented data, and OCR baselines, while inference relies on page images and PDF-parser interleaved text-image inputs rather than external OCR.
Rationale / Key Technique: Trains a native long-context document MLLM on interleaved text-image data, emphasizing backbone adaptation rather than retrieval augmentation.

OCR Use: No OCR is used; the VLM predicts evidence pages and answers from visual document inputs.
Rationale / Key Technique: Fine-tunes a VLM with evidence-page-guided GRPO so the model jointly predicts supporting pages and answers from visual document inputs.

OCR Use: No OCR is used in the proposed method; OCR appears only in baseline comparisons while DocReact reasons over multi-page document images and multimodal evidence.
Rationale / Key Technique: Uses an iterative ReAct-style process with subquery generation, retrieval, judging, and final answer synthesis over heterogeneous document evidence.

OCR Use: OCR-dependent: a lightweight OCR module provides word-box tokens that are fused with layout and visual crop features for compression and streaming inference.
Rationale / Key Technique: Uses OCR, layout, and visual features inside a small VLM with hierarchical multimodal compression and streaming abstention for long-document inference.

OCR Use: OCR-dependent: it extends OCR/text-layout document VQA backbones with global document tokens and local-global reasoning.
Rationale / Key Technique: Adds global document tokens and local-global reasoning modules to fuse page-level and document-level multimodal context.

OCR Use: OCR-dependent: per-page OCR/text-layout representations are encoded before hierarchical page-summary reasoning.
Rationale / Key Technique: Encodes each page and then reasons over page summary tokens, making hierarchical page-to-document fusion the central design.

OCR Use: OCR-dependent: an external OCR engine supplies OCR tokens and bounding boxes to the Document-former/LLM along with image features.
Rationale / Key Technique: Uses the InstructDoc instruction mixture and a Document-former bridge to adapt a backbone across visual document tasks.

OCR Use: No OCR is specified; it operates over already available passages, pages, tables, and graph structure rather than an OCR pipeline.
Rationale / Key Technique: Converts parsed passages, pages, and tables into a knowledge graph, then uses graph traversal and prompting to retrieve structured evidence.

OCR Use: OCR-dependent: OCR text segments and coordinates are the primary model input, with layout tokens adding spatial information.
Rationale / Key Technique: Treats OCR text segments as the main input and injects layout through a compact layout token sharing text positions; key technique is model-internal layout-token adaptation and layout-aware pretraining.

OCR Use: No external OCR is used; text-rich reading is handled internally by the high-resolution multi-image VLM.
Rationale / Key Technique: Adapts a text-rich multi-image VLM through high-resolution visual encoding and instruction tuning, avoiding an explicit OCR pipeline.

OCR Use: Uses OCR/PDF parsing for the textual retrieval branch, but is not solely OCR-dependent because it also preserves page images and uses visual retrieval plus visual extraction agents.
Rationale / Key Technique: Combines BM25 and visual retrieval with text filter, visual extractor, and modal fuser agents for multimodal RAG.

OCR Use: No OCR is used; it explicitly uses page-image retrieval and VLM generation instead of text extraction/OCR.
Rationale / Key Technique: Uses visual document retrieval, such as page-image embeddings, followed by VLM answer generation for multi-page and multi-document QA.

OCR Use: No OCR is used; VLM agents operate over visual document inputs with LLM-based judgment and answer synthesis.
Rationale / Key Technique: Uses VLM-based planning and execution agents plus LLM-based judgment and answer agents, with adaptive test-time scaling and self-correction.

OCR Use: Uses OCR/PDF parsing to build the text retrieval branch, but is not fully OCR-dependent because it runs parallel image retrieval and dedicated image agents over preserved page images.
Rationale / Key Technique: Combines text and image RAG with general, critical, text, image, and summarizer agents to coordinate multimodal evidence use.

OCR Use: No OCR is used in the proposed method; it uses Docling PDF parsing, hierarchical text indexes, LVLM visual descriptions, and raw visual evidence, while OCR appears only in baselines.
Rationale / Key Technique: Builds hierarchical, multi-granularity indexes over visual-rich documents and retrieves parent pages, summaries, and chunks before generation.

OCR Use: OCR-dependent: OCR/MinerU-derived chunks, including OCR-derived table Markdown, are part of the retrieval units used for LVLM generation.
Rationale / Key Technique: Constructs a multimodal chunk-query graph with synthetic query anchors, then retrieves and expands evidence through graph links.

OCR Use: No OCR is used in the proposed method; OCR appears in text-RAG baselines and OCR-method ablations only.
Rationale / Key Technique: Uses multimodal logic-aware retrieval over a page graph, combining semantic similarity with graph traversal before generation.

OCR Use: No OCR is used; it explicitly targets OCR-free multi-page document understanding from compressed high-resolution page images.
Rationale / Key Technique: Compresses high-resolution page images into compact visual tokens and trains an OCR-free multi-page MLLM.

OCR Use: OCR-dependent: OCR over detected regions is part of the parsing/chunking pipeline used to construct structure-aware multimodal RAG units.
Rationale / Key Technique: Uses document parsing, OCR, and structure-aware multimodal chunking to build retrieval units for long industrial-document RAG.

OCR Use: Uses parsed/OCR-token text when available through the PDF parsing pipeline, but is not OCR-dependent because sparse sampling operates over interleaved text and image evidence and can select visual content.
Rationale / Key Technique: Parses both text and images, then applies sparse sampling and shared visual encoding to support efficient long-PDF reading.

OCR Use: Uses OCR in the textual RAG variants, but is not OCR-dependent as a model family because it also evaluates visual OCR-free RAG variants.
Rationale / Key Technique: Studies both textual and visual RAG variants, using retrieval over OCR/text or visual evidence before a document VQA generator.

OCR Use: OCR-dependent: OCR word and spatial embeddings are consumed with visual features while recurrent memory carries information across pages.
Rationale / Key Technique: Carries recurrent memory tokens across pages while consuming multimodal page inputs, enabling document-level reasoning over long page sequences.

OCR Use: No OCR is used; Pix2Struct-style visual representations and self-attention select the relevant page image.
Rationale / Key Technique: Uses Pix2Struct visual representations and self-attention scoring to select the most relevant page image before answering.

OCR Use: Uses extracted page text as an auxiliary cue together with page images and VLM-generated summaries, but is not OCR-dependent because retrieval and reasoning are primarily visual and memory-guided.
Rationale / Key Technique: Combines visual page retrieval with textual page summaries, LLM reranking, memory, and iterative query refinement.

OCR Use: Uses OCR during training/data construction and is trained for OCR tasks, but it is not dependent on an external OCR engine at inference because OCR is an internal VLM capability.
Rationale / Key Technique: Uses token-efficient high-resolution visual compression and grounding-oriented training to read documents without external OCR.

OCR Use: Uses OCR text only as a pretraining pseudo-target/signal; inference is not OCR-dependent and operates through visual document retrieval and generation over page images.
Rationale / Key Technique: Adapts LVLMs into a visual document retriever and generator, using OCR only as a pretraining signal while inference operates on page images.

OCR Use: Uses OCR text chunks in the textual retrieval pipeline, but is not fully OCR-dependent because it also uses visual retrieval and dynamic agentic inspection over images.
Rationale / Key Technique: Combines textual and visual retrieval through GMM-based adaptive recall, then uses seeker, inspector, and answer agents for iterative reasoning.
